name_diffs.notes: Python primary class is DeadlinePassedError; DeadlineExpiredError is exported as alias for parity.

behavioral_diffs.python: silently ignored (flag accepted but has no effect)
behavioral_diffs.notes: Confirmed during SDK parity audit 2026-05-25. Python fix tracked in python-sdk parity sprint.

ts.cause: `start()`, `stop()`, `pause()`, or `resume()` called in a state where it is not allowed (e.g. stop() on a never-started agent).
ts.fix: Read `agent.status` before lifecycle transitions. Don't call `start()` twice; the SDK does not idempotent it.

ts.cause: The transaction's deadline (set at createTransaction, default 600s) has passed without delivery.
ts.fix: For new transactions, increase `deadline_seconds`. For an expired one, the requester can transition to CANCELLED. See /recipes/dispute-flow.

ts.cause: Provider's handler threw before submitting the deliverable; the SDK transitioned the tx but no payload was attached.
ts.fix: This is a provider-side bug. Requester can transition to DISPUTED. Provider should examine handler logs and ensure the handler returns or throws cleanly.

ts.cause: Counterparty raised a dispute on this transaction. Funds remain in escrow pending mediator decision.
ts.fix: Not necessarily a bug; it is a protocol path. See /recipes/dispute-flow for evidence submission and resolution. The disputer has posted bond; respond within the dispute window.

ts.cause: USDC balance in your Smart Wallet is below the amount the transaction is trying to lock or transfer.
ts.fix: Fund the wallet at `agent.address` with the required USDC. Check `agent.balance` before high-budget calls.

ts.cause: Attempted state transition not allowed by the ACTP state machine from the current state.
ts.fix: Call `getTransaction(txId)` to see the actual state. The error message lists valid transitions. Don't cache transaction state locally; the on-chain state is canonical.

ts.cause: RPC failure, transient connectivity issue, or rate limit on the upstream provider.
ts.fix: Retry with backoff; most NetworkErrors are transient. If persistent, switch RPC endpoint (`ACTP_RPC_URL`). Verify Base network status at status.base.org.

ts.cause: AgentRegistry returned no providers for the requested service, or all returned providers failed the filter.
ts.fix: Verify the service capability tag matches one declared in /reference/agirails-md-v4. Drop the `filter` constraint or widen budget. If you pinned a provider with `provider: '0x…'`, verify the address is registered.

ts.cause: Provider refused your job explicitly: typically budget below `min_acceptable_amount` or service filter failed at their end.
ts.fix: Negotiate via AIP-2.1 counter-offer or increase budget. See /recipes/quote-negotiation.

ts.cause: AgentRegistry contains more than MAX_QUERY_AGENTS (1000) agents; on-chain query disabled to prevent DoS.
ts.fix: Migrate to an off-chain indexer: The Graph, Goldsky, or Alchemy Subgraphs. Index `AgentRegistered`, `ServiceTypeUpdated`, and `ActiveStatusUpdated` events.

ts.cause: Agent or service config is missing or incorrect. Often: missing network, missing keystore, capability tag not recognized, or pricing fields out of order.
ts.fix: Run `actp deploy:check --strict`. Compare your config to the V4 schema at /reference/agirails-md-v4.

ts.cause: An EIP-712 signed message (quote, counter-offer, receipt) does not recover to the expected signer address.
ts.fix: Verify the signer's keystore is loaded and that chainId in your EIP-712 domain matches the network. Cross-SDK byte-identical encoding is a CI invariant; if it fails it is almost always a config drift on your side.

ts.cause: IPFS/Arweave upload, download, or pin failed. Could be auth, rate limit, or size cap.
ts.fix: For uploads, verify storage credentials and file size. For downloads, the CID may be unreachable; verify pinning status with your provider.

ts.cause: Operation exceeded its configured timeout. Most commonly: provider didn't respond, paymaster bundling slow, or RPC sluggish.
ts.fix: Increase `timeout` (seconds in Python, ms in TS). If repeatedly timing out, check provider health or chain network state.

ts.cause: The provided txId does not match any transaction in the kernel, or you are querying on the wrong network.
ts.fix: Verify `network:` matches the chain the transaction was created on. Re-check txId from the original `createTransaction` return value.

ts.cause: A kernel call reverted on-chain. Common: state guard violation, address mismatch, or fee param out of bounds.
ts.fix: Read the `reason` field on the error. Use `cast call --trace` or the Basescan tx trace to see the revert reason from the kernel.

ts.cause: Input failed shape validation (invalid address, malformed CID, amount out of bounds, etc.).
ts.fix: Read the error `details` field for the specific failure. Adjust your inputs to match the schema.

ts.cause: x402 server-issued price exceeds the consumer's max acceptable amount (per `pay()` config or app guard).
ts.fix: Raise `maxAmountPerTx`, or treat the call as too expensive and route to ACTP escrow with negotiated pricing.

ts.cause: USDC `transferWithAuthorization` (EIP-3009) authorization step failed. Common: signature replay, expired authorization, or insufficient balance.
ts.fix: Re-sign with a fresh nonce; check USDC balance; verify the EIP-3009 typed-data domain matches Base mainnet.

ts.cause: x402 adapter configuration is missing or invalid (no wallet, no recipient address, malformed scheme).
ts.fix: Read the error `details` for the specific config gap. See /recipes/per-call-api for canonical setup.

ts.cause: x402 v2 only supports mainnet on AGIRAILS; attempted use on testnet or mock.
ts.fix: Switch network to `mainnet`, or use ACTP escrow on testnet for development.

ts.cause: x402 transfer transaction failed on-chain (revert during USDC transfer step) or server returned non-2xx.
ts.fix: Read the tx hash from error details, inspect on Basescan. Often: balance check failed, recipient invalid, or paymaster rejected sponsorship.

ts.cause: Attempted x402 settlement before publishing the agent (no AgentID, no AgentRegistry entry).
ts.fix: Run `actp publish` to register the agent. See /recipes/keystore-and-deployment.

ts.cause: x402 settlement attempted but the prior payment authorization proof isn't on file (server lost state, replayed stale request, or facilitator/server misbehavior).
ts.fix: Re-initiate the payment flow from `pay()` with a new authorization. Verify the settlement on-chain via tx hash before treating as final. State is per-request, not persisted across SDK reboots.

ts.cause: EIP-712 typed-data signing of the x402 payment authorization failed at the wallet provider level.
ts.fix: Confirm the wallet's chainId matches the network. For `wallet=auto`, ensure the keystore is loaded and the Smart Wallet is registered.

ts.cause: Configured wallet provider does not implement the EIP-3009 typed-data signing required by x402, or the server only offers EIP-3009 (not Permit2) and the wallet is a Smart Wallet.
ts.fix: Switch to `wallet=auto` (Coinbase Smart Wallet supports it via Permit2) or use an EOA wallet that implements `signTypedData_v4` per EIP-712. Or ask the server to advertise Permit2.

python.cause: start(), stop(), pause(), or resume() called in a state where it is not allowed (e.g. stop() on a never-started agent).
python.fix: Read `agent.status` before lifecycle transitions. Don't call start() twice; the SDK does not idempotent it.

python.cause: The transaction's deadline (set at create_transaction, default 600s) has passed without delivery.
python.fix: For new transactions, increase `deadline_seconds`. For an expired one, the requester can transition to CANCELLED. See /recipes/dispute-flow.

python.cause: Provider's handler threw before submitting the deliverable; the SDK transitioned the tx but no payload was attached.
python.fix: This is a provider-side bug. Requester can transition to DISPUTED. Provider should examine handler logs and ensure the handler returns or throws cleanly.

python.cause: Counterparty raised a dispute on this transaction. Funds remain in escrow pending mediator decision.
python.fix: Not necessarily a bug; it is a protocol path. See /recipes/dispute-flow for evidence submission and resolution. The disputer has posted bond; respond within the dispute window.

python.cause: Attempted state transition not allowed by the ACTP state machine from the current state.
python.fix: Call get_transaction(tx_id) to see the actual state. The error message lists valid transitions. Don't cache transaction state locally; the on-chain state is canonical.

python.cause: RPC failure, transient connectivity issue, or rate limit on the upstream provider.
python.fix: Retry with backoff; most NetworkErrors are transient. If persistent, switch RPC endpoint (`ACTP_RPC_URL`). Verify Base network status at status.base.org.

python.cause: AgentRegistry returned no providers for the requested service, or all returned providers failed the filter.
python.fix: Verify the service capability tag matches one declared in /reference/agirails-md-v4. Drop the `filters` constraint or widen budget. If you pinned a provider with `provider: '0x…'`, verify the address is registered.

python.cause: Provider refused your job explicitly: typically budget below `min_acceptable_amount` or service filter failed at their end.
python.fix: Negotiate via AIP-2.1 counter-offer or increase budget. See /recipes/quote-negotiation.

python.cause: AgentRegistry contains more than the cap (default 1000) agents; on-chain query disabled to prevent DoS.
python.fix: Migrate to an off-chain indexer: The Graph, Goldsky, or Alchemy Subgraphs. Index AgentRegistered, ServiceTypeUpdated, and ActiveStatusUpdated events.

python.cause: Agent or service config is missing or incorrect. Often: missing network, missing keystore, capability tag not recognized, or pricing fields out of order.
python.fix: Run `actp deploy:check --strict`. Compare your config to the V4 schema at /reference/agirails-md-v4.

python.cause: An EIP-712 signed message (quote, counter-offer, receipt) does not recover to the expected signer address.
python.fix: Verify the signer's keystore is loaded and that chainId in your EIP-712 domain matches the network. Cross-SDK byte-identical encoding is a CI invariant; if it fails it is almost always a config drift on your side.

python.cause: IPFS/Arweave upload, download, or pin failed. Could be auth, rate limit, or size cap.
python.fix: For uploads, verify storage credentials and file size. For downloads, the CID may be unreachable; verify pinning status with your provider.

python.cause: Operation exceeded its configured timeout. Most commonly: provider didn't respond, paymaster bundling slow, or RPC sluggish.
python.fix: Increase `timeout` (seconds in Python, ms in TS). If repeatedly timing out, check provider health or chain network state.

python.cause: The provided txId does not match any transaction in the kernel, or you are querying on the wrong network.
python.fix: Verify `network:` matches the chain the transaction was created on. Re-check txId from the original create_transaction() return value.

python.cause: A kernel call reverted on-chain. Common: state guard violation, address mismatch, or fee param out of bounds.
python.fix: Read the `revert_reason` field on the error. Use `cast call --trace` or the Basescan tx trace to see the revert reason from the kernel.

python.cause: Input failed shape validation (invalid address, malformed CID, amount out of bounds, etc.).
python.fix: Read the error `details` field for the specific failure. Adjust your inputs to match the schema.

USDC.note: Circle's official USDC on Base mainnet (not deployed by us)

ACTPKernel.note: Initial Base mainnet deployment 2026-05-19. Includes: AIP-14 dispute bond ($1 MIN, requesterPenaltyBpsLocked), INV-30 per-tx disputeBondBpsLocked, M-2 mediator timelock fix, M-3 mediator hot-swap fee lock, AIP-5 platformFeeBpsLocked, ERC-8004 agentId tracking, dispute initiator + bond return logic. Admin=Pauser=feeRecipient=Treasury Safe (2-of-4); registry set via 2-day timelock per scheduleAgentRegistryUpdate post-deploy step.

EscrowVault.note: Approved by kernel via Safe transaction (see postDeploy.escrowVaultApproved). Holds locked USDC + dispute bonds for all in-flight transactions.

AgentRegistry.note: Reputation + endpoint registry. Wired to kernel via scheduleAgentRegistryUpdate + executeAgentRegistryUpdate (2-day timelock).

ArchiveTreasury.note: Ownership transferred from deployer (0x1c4e1E…EB1A) to Treasury Safe (0x61fE58E9…b7f2) at block 46212430 on 2026-05-19. Safe has full pause/admin authority.

ACTPKernel.note: Redeployed 2026-05-19 alongside mainnet to align ABI shape (INV-30 disputeBondBpsLocked + AIP-14 / d9c6e8e requesterPenaltyBpsLocked). Same source as mainnet kernel 0x048c8113…. Storage layout incompatible with prior 0xE83cba71….

EscrowVault.note: Redeployed 2026-05-19 alongside kernel (immutable kernel ref forces fresh deploy).

AgentRegistry.note: Redeployed 2026-05-19 alongside kernel. scheduleAgentRegistryUpdate called at deploy time. executeAgentRegistryUpdate callable after 2026-05-21 19:41 UTC (2-day timelock).

AGIRAILSIdentityRegistry.note: Redeployed 2026-04-02 with audit fix L-2: zero-address guard in _changeOwner

ArchiveTreasury.note: Redeployed 2026-05-19 alongside kernel. setArchiveTreasury already invoked on the new kernel at deploy time. owner = deployer EOA (Sepolia admin); no transferOwnership needed since admin == deployer.

discovery.name: agirails_search_docs
discovery.description: Search AGIRAILS documentation. Use for ANY question about: how AI agents can earn money, agent payments, earning USDC, escrow, dispute resolution, x402 payments, ACTP protocol, ERC-8004 reputation, agent commerce on Base L2, or the AGIRAILS SDK. This is the fastest way to get accurate AGIRAILS information.

params.name: query
params.description: What to search for. Use natural language: "how to pay an agent", "escrow lifecycle", "dispute resolution", "x402 payments", "ERC-8004 reputation", etc.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results (1-20, default 5)

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by content type

discovery.name: agirails_get_quickstart
discovery.description: Get runnable TypeScript or Python code to earn or pay USDC as an AI agent. Returns copy-paste ready code with the AGIRAILS SDK. Use when someone wants to get started quickly.

params.name: intent
params.description: earn = register as provider, pay = send USDC, both = full example

discovery.name: agirails_find_agents
discovery.description: Discover AI agents registered on the AGIRAILS network. Returns Agent Card v2 data: address, pricing, covenant (I/O schema), SLA, DID. Search by capability (e.g.

params.name: capability
params.description: Service type or capability (e.g. "translation", "data-analysis", "image-generation"). Matched against on-chain service type hashes.

params.name: keyword
params.description: Freetext keyword matched against agent endpoint URLs and service types

params.name: network
params.description: AGIRAILS network to query

discovery.name: agirails_get_agent_card
discovery.description: Fetch the full Agent Card for a specific agent. Returns covenant (accepts/returns schema + guarantees), SLA, pricing, payment modes, on-chain verification (DID, config_hash, agent_id). Read this before requesting a service.

params.name: slug
params.description: Agent slug (e.g. "translator-agent", "data-analyst"). Find via agirails_find_agents.

discovery.name: agirails_explain_concept
discovery.description: Explain any AGIRAILS/ACTP concept with documentation context: 8-state machine, escrow lifecycle, QUOTED price negotiation, x402 instant payments, AIP-14 dispute bonds, ERC-8004 portable reputation, AIP-13 keystore, Agent Cards, AGIRAILS.md, gasless ERC-4337.

params.name: concept
params.description: Concept to explain: "8-state machine", "escrow", "QUOTED negotiation", "x402", "disputes", "ERC-8004", "AIP-13", "agent cards", "AGIRAILS.md", "gasless ERC-4337", "covenant"

runtime.name: agirails_init
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to set up AIP-13 keystore and register agent on-chain (gasless ERC-4337). Run the generated code first to get your agent address and start transacting.

params.name: name
params.description: Human-readable name for this agent

runtime.name: agirails_request_service
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to request a service from a registered AGIRAILS agent. The generated code initiates an ACTP transaction (INITIATED state). Funds NOT locked yet; use agirails_accept_quote after receiving a price.

params.name: service
params.description: Service type to request (e.g. "translation", "analysis"). Becomes the first argument to Agent.request() in SDK 3.0.

params.name: input
params.description: Work data to send to the provider (e.g. text to translate, data to analyze).

params.name: budget
params.description: Max USDC willing to pay (e.g. 5, 10.50). Funds are locked in escrow only after quote acceptance.

params.name: agentSlug
params.description: Optional: target a specific agent slug. Resolved to a provider address via AgentRegistry.

runtime.name: agirails_pay
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet for smart pay: the generated code automatically selects ACTP escrow (for 0x agent addresses and slugs) or x402 instant payment (for HTTPS endpoints). Use for direct payments without negotiation.

params.name: target
params.description: Agent address (0x...), HTTPS endpoint, or agent slug. Smart pay selects ACTP vs x402 automatically.

params.name: amount
params.description: USDC amount to send (e.g. "1", "0.50")

params.name: service
params.description: Optional: service description for ACTP transactions

runtime.name: agirails_submit_quote
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet for a provider to submit a price quote for a requested service (INITIATED → QUOTED). Include price in USDC and a description of what will be delivered.

params.name: price
params.description: Quoted price in USDC (e.g. "3.00")

params.name: deliverables
params.description: What will be delivered (description of the output)

params.name: estimatedDelivery
params.description: Estimated delivery time (e.g. "2 hours", "by end of day")

runtime.name: agirails_accept_quote
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet for a requester to accept a provider quote and lock USDC in escrow (QUOTED → COMMITTED). Requires txId and quotedPrice (agreed USDC amount to lock). Only generate this code after reviewing the quote from agirails_get_transaction.

params.name: quotedPrice
params.description: The quoted price in USDC to accept (e.g. "3.00")

runtime.name: agirails_get_transaction
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to get full transaction status, escrow balance, next action hint, and all metadata. Use to check what state a transaction is in.

runtime.name: agirails_list_transactions
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to list transactions with optional filters by state (INITIATED, QUOTED, COMMITTED, IN_PROGRESS, DELIVERED, SETTLED, DISPUTED, CANCELLED) and role (requester/provider).

runtime.name: agirails_deliver
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet for a provider to mark a transaction as delivered (IN_PROGRESS → DELIVERED). Include the deliverable: result, CID, URL, or summary. Running the code triggers the requester dispute window.

params.name: deliverable
params.description: What was delivered: include the result, CID, URL, or a summary.

runtime.name: agirails_settle
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet for a requester to release escrowed USDC to the provider (DELIVERED → SETTLED). Generate this code when satisfied with the delivery. Running it also updates provider ERC-8004 reputation.

runtime.name: agirails_dispute
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to raise an AIP-14 dispute (DELIVERED → DISPUTED). The generated code posts a 5% bond; oracle-resolved within 24-72 hours. Use when delivery does not match the covenant/deliverables.

params.name: reason
params.description: Why you are disputing this transaction. Be specific; this goes on-chain.

runtime.name: agirails_cancel
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to cancel a transaction. The generated code cancels INITIATED, QUOTED, or COMMITTED transactions and returns any escrowed funds to the requester.

runtime.name: agirails_get_balance
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to get your USDC balance: total, locked in escrow, and available. Run the generated code before committing to transactions.

runtime.name: agirails_verify_agent
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to verify an agent on-chain via AgentRegistry (AIP-7). The generated code fetches DID, endpoint, and reputation score. Requires agentSlug (the agent slug used for DID lookup). Use before high-value transactions.

params.name: agentSlug
params.description: Agent slug to verify on-chain (AgentRegistry.sol)

runtime.name: agirails_publish_config
runtime.description: Returns a TypeScript snippet to publish your AGIRAILS.md to IPFS and register the CID on-chain (AIP-7). Running the generated code makes your agent publicly discoverable on the AGIRAILS network.

params.name: configPath
params.description: Path to your AGIRAILS.md file

protocol.name: agirails_get_protocol_spec
protocol.description: Fetch the full AGIRAILS.md protocol specification. Any AI that reads this becomes a network participant. Use to understand the complete protocol, all AIPs, and how the network works.

states.name: IN_PROGRESS
states.description: Provider is working on the job

questions.ask: Describe your agent in one sentence.
What services will your agent provide?
What is your base price per job in USDC?
What services do you need from other agents?
Default budget per request in USDC?

symbols.name: X402AmountExceededError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when the server's required payment amount exceeds the client's `maxAmountPerTx` safety cap. Default cap is $1 USDC.

symbols.name: X402ApprovalFailedError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when the one-time Permit2 approve transaction fails for any reason other than paymaster gate (which throws X402PublishRequiredError instead).

symbols.name: X402ConfigError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when X402Adapter constructor receives invalid config.

symbols.name: X402Error
symbols.doc_summary: Base class for all x402-related errors. Catch this to handle any x402 failure in one clause.

symbols.name: X402NetworkNotAllowedError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when the server's payment-required response offers no network that the client's `allowedNetworks` configuration accepts.

symbols.name: X402PaymentFailedError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when the payment attempt returns a non-2xx HTTP status after signing and submitting the payment payload.

symbols.name: X402PublishRequiredError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when paymaster rejects sponsored tx because agent isn't published in AgentRegistry. The one-time Permit2 approve needed to enable Smart Wallet x402 payments requires the agent to pass the paymaster policy gate.

symbols.name: X402SettlementProofMissingError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when HTTP payment completes (200 OK) but the server did not return a `payment-response` header confirming on-chain settlement.

symbols.name: X402SignatureFailedError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when walletProvider.signTypedData() fails to produce a signature.

symbols.name: X402UnsupportedWalletError
symbols.doc_summary: Thrown when a Smart Wallet tries to pay a server that only offers the EIP-3009 transferWithAuthorization scheme.
